          V-223296 - Add-on Management must be enabled for all Office 365 ProPlus programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223296r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000014
          Identities:SV-108771
          V-99667
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: Internet Explorer add-ons are pieces of code, run in Internet Explorer, to provide additional functionality. Rogue add-ons may contain viruses or other malicious code. Disabling or not configuring this setting could allow malicious code or users to become active on user computers or the network. For example, a malicious user can monitor and then use keystrokes that user's type into Internet Explorer. Even legitimate add-ons may demand resources, compromising the performance of Internet Explorer and the operating systems for user computers.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security "Add-on Management" is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main\FeatureControl\FEATURE_ADDON_MANAGEMENT

          If the value for each installed Office Program is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security "Add-on Management" to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223296
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000014 - Add-on Management must be enabled for all Office 365 ProPlus programs.
          Description:Internet Explorer add-ons are pieces of code, run in Internet Explorer, to provide additional functionality. Rogue add-ons may contain viruses or other malicious code. Disabling or not configuring this setting could allow malicious code or users to become active on user computers or the network. For example, a malicious user can monitor and then use keystrokes that user's type into Internet Explorer. Even legitimate add-ons may demand resources, compromising the performance of Internet Explorer and the operating systems for user computers.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for SharePoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Add-on Management' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Exchange)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22329600 (registry_test)

          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          V-223297 - Consistent MIME handling must be enabled for all Office 365 ProPlus programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223297r961050_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000015
          Identities:SV-108773
          V-99669
          CCI-000803 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-7.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-7)
          Description: Encryption is only as good as the encryption modules utilized. Unapproved cryptographic module algorithms cannot be verified and cannot be relied on to provide confidentiality or integrity, and DoD data may be compromised due to weak algorithms. The use of TLS provides confidentiality of data in transit between the application server and client. FIPS 140-2 approved TLS versions include TLS V1.0 or greater.

          TLS must be enabled and non-FIPS-approved SSL versions must be disabled. NIST SP 800-52 specifies the preferred configurations for government systems.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Consistent Mime Handling is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_mime_handling

          If the value for all installed Office programs is set to is REG_DWORD=1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security "Consistent Mime Handling" to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223297
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000015 - Consistent MIME handling must be enabled for all Office 365 ProPlus programs.
          Description:Encryption is only as good as the encryption modules utilized. Unapproved cryptographic module algorithms cannot be verified and cannot be relied on to provide confidentiality or integrity, and DoD data may be compromised due to weak algorithms. The use of TLS provides confidentiality of data in transit between the application server and client. FIPS 140-2 approved TLS versions include TLS V1.0 or greater.

          TLS must be enabled and non-FIPS-approved SSL versions must be disabled. NIST SP 800-52 specifies the preferred configurations for government systems.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for SharePoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Exchange)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22329700 (registry_test)

          V-223298 - User name and password must be disabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223298r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000016
          Identities:SV-108775
          V-99671
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: The Uniform Resource Locator (URL) standard allows user authentication to be included in URL strings in the form http://username:password@example.com. A malicious user might use this URL syntax to create a hyperlink that appears to open a legitimate website but actually opens a deceptive (spoofed) website. For example, the URL http://www.wingtiptoys.com@example.com appears to open http://www.wingtiptoys.com but actually opens http://example.com. To protect users from such attacks, Internet Explorer usually blocks any URLs using this syntax.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security "Disable user name and password" is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main\FeatureControl\FEATURE_HTTP_USERNAME_PASSWORD_DISABLE

          If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security "Disable user name and password" to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223298
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000016 - User name and password must be disabled in all Office programs.
          Description:The Uniform Resource Locator (URL) standard allows user authentication to be included in URL strings in the form http://username:password@example.com. A malicious user might use this URL syntax to create a hyperlink that appears to open a legitimate website but actually opens a deceptive (spoofed) website. For example, the URL http://www.wingtiptoys.com@example.com appears to open http://www.wingtiptoys.com but actually opens http://example.com. To protect users from such attacks, Internet Explorer usually blocks any URLs using this syntax.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Disable user name and password' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Disable user name and password' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Disable user name and password' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)

              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))

          V-223299 - The Information Bar must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223299r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000017
          Identities:SV-108777
          V-99673
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether Office 365 ProPlus applications notify users when potentially unsafe features or content are detected, or whether such features or content are silently disabled without notification. The Message Bar in Office 2016 applications is used to identify security issues, such as unsigned macros or potentially unsafe add-ins. When such issues are detected, the application disables the unsafe feature or content and displays the Message Bar at the top of the active window. The Message Bar informs the users about the nature of the security issue and, in some cases, provides the users with an option to enable the potentially unsafe feature or content, which could harm the user's computer.

          If you enable this policy setting, Office 365 ProPlus applications do not display information in the Message Bar about potentially unsafe content that has been detected or has automatically been blocked.

          If you disable this policy setting, Office 365 ProPlus applications display information in the Message Bar about content that has automatically been blocked.

          If you do not configure this policy setting, if an Office 365 ProPlus application detects a security issue, the Message Bar is displayed. However, this configuration can be modified by users in the Trust Center.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Information Bar is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_securityband

          If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Information Bar to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223299
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000017 - The Information Bar must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether Office 365 ProPlus applications notify users when potentially unsafe features or content are detected, or whether such features or content are silently disabled without notification. The Message Bar in Office 2016 applications is used to identify security issues, such as unsigned macros or potentially unsafe add-ins. When such issues are detected, the application disables the unsafe feature or content and displays the Message Bar at the top of the active window. The Message Bar informs the users about the nature of the security issue and, in some cases, provides the users with an option to enable the potentially unsafe feature or content, which could harm the user's computer.

          If you enable this policy setting, Office 365 ProPlus applications do not display information in the Message Bar about potentially unsafe content that has been detected or has automatically been blocked.

          If you disable this policy setting, Office 365 ProPlus applications display information in the Message Bar about content that has automatically been blocked.

          If you do not configure this policy setting, if an Office 365 ProPlus application detects a security issue, the Message Bar is displayed. However, this configuration can be modified by users in the Trust Center.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Groove)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Powerpoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Sharepoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Powerpoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (Information Bar is set to 'Enabled' for Exchange)

          V-223300 - The Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223300r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000018
          Identities:SV-108779
          V-99675
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Internet Explorer places restrictions on each web page users can use the browser to open. Web pages on a user's local computer have the fewest security restrictions and reside in the Local Machine zone, making this security zone a prime target for malicious users and code. Disabling or not configuring this setting could allow pages in the Internet zone to navigate to pages in the Local Machine zone to then run code to elevate privileges. This could allow malicious code or users to become active on user computers or the network.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_localmachine_lockdown

          If the value for all installed Office programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Local Machine Zone Lockdown to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223300
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000018 - The Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:Internet Explorer places restrictions on each web page users can use the browser to open. Web pages on a user's local computer have the fewest security restrictions and reside in the Local Machine zone, making this security zone a prime target for malicious users and code. Disabling or not configuring this setting could allow pages in the Internet zone to navigate to pages in the Local Machine zone to then run code to elevate privileges. This could allow malicious code or users to become active on user computers or the network.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for SharePoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Local Machine Zone Lockdown Security' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Exchange)

          V-223301 - The MIME Sniffing safety feature must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223301r961050_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000019
          Identities:SV-108781
          V-99677
          CCI-000803 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-7.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-7)
          Description: Encryption is only as good as the encryption modules utilized. Unapproved cryptographic module algorithms cannot be verified and cannot be relied upon to provide confidentiality or integrity, and DoD data may be compromised due to weak algorithms. The use of TLS provides confidentiality of data in transit between the application server and client. FIPS 140-2 approved TLS versions include TLS V1.0 or greater.

          TLS must be enabled and non-FIPS-approved SSL versions must be disabled. NIST SP 800-52 specifies the preferred configurations for government systems.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Mime Sniffing Safety Feature is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_mime_sniffing

          If the value for all installed Office Programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Mime Sniffing Safety Feature to "Enabled" for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223301
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000019 - The MIME Sniffing safety feature must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:Encryption is only as good as the encryption modules utilized. Unapproved cryptographic module algorithms cannot be verified and cannot be relied upon to provide confidentiality or integrity, and DoD data may be compromised due to weak algorithms. The use of TLS provides confidentiality of data in transit between the application server and client. FIPS 140-2 approved TLS versions include TLS V1.0 or greater.

          TLS must be enabled and non-FIPS-approved SSL versions must be disabled. NIST SP 800-52 specifies the preferred configurations for government systems.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for SharePoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Exchange)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22330100 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:IE Security 'Mime Sniffing Safety Feature' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22330100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Access
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main\FeatureControl\FEATURE_MIME_SNIFFING'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'msaccess.exe'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main\FeatureControl\FEATURE_MIME_SNIFFING\msaccess.exe'. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223302 - Navigate URL must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223302r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000020
          Identities:SV-108783
          V-99679
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: To protect users from attacks, Internet Explorer usually does not attempt to load malformed URLs. This functionality can be controlled separately for instances of Internet Explorer spawned by Office applications (for example, if a user clicks a link in an Office document or selects a menu option that loads a web page). If Internet Explorer attempts to load a malformed URL, a security risk could occur.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Navigate URL is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_validate_navigate_url

          If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Navigate URL to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223302
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000020 - Navigate URL must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:To protect users from attacks, Internet Explorer usually does not attempt to load malformed URLs. This functionality can be controlled separately for instances of Internet Explorer spawned by Office applications (for example, if a user clicks a link in an Office document or selects a menu option that loads a web page). If Internet Explorer attempts to load a malformed URL, a security risk could occur.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for SharePoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Navigate URL' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Exchange)

          V-223303 - Object Caching Protection must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223303r961050_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000021
          Identities:SV-108785
          V-99681
          CCI-000803 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-7.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-7)
          Description: Encryption is only as good as the encryption modules utilized. Unapproved cryptographic module algorithms cannot be verified and cannot be relied upon to provide confidentiality or integrity, and DoD data may be compromised due to weak algorithms. The use of TLS provides confidentiality of data in transit between the application server and client. FIPS 140-2 approved TLS versions include TLS V1.0 or greater.

          TLS must be enabled and non-FIPS-approved SSL versions must be disabled. NIST SP 800-52 specifies the preferred configurations for government systems.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Object Caching Protection is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_object_caching

          If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Object Caching Protection to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223303
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000021 - Object Caching Protection must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:Encryption is only as good as the encryption modules utilized. Unapproved cryptographic module algorithms cannot be verified and cannot be relied upon to provide confidentiality or integrity, and DoD data may be compromised due to weak algorithms. The use of TLS provides confidentiality of data in transit between the application server and client. FIPS 140-2 approved TLS versions include TLS V1.0 or greater.

          TLS must be enabled and non-FIPS-approved SSL versions must be disabled. NIST SP 800-52 specifies the preferred configurations for government systems.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Groove)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Powerpoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Sharepoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Powerpoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (Object Caching Protection is set to 'Enabled' for Exchange)

          V-223304 - Protection from zone elevation must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223304r960921_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000022
          Identities:SV-108787
          V-99683
          CCI-001695 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (3); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (3).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (3); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (3))
          Description: Internet Explorer places restrictions on each web page users can use the browser to open. Web pages on a user's local computer have the fewest security restrictions and reside in the Local Machine zone, making this security zone a prime target for malicious users and code. Disabling or not configuring this setting could allow pages in the Internet zone to navigate to pages in the Local Machine zone to then run code to elevate privileges. This could allow malicious code or users to become active on user computers or the network.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Protection from Zone Elevation is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_zone_elevation

          If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Protection from Zone Elevation to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223304
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000022 - Protection from zone elevation must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:Internet Explorer places restrictions on each web page users can use the browser to open. Web pages on a user's local computer have the fewest security restrictions and reside in the Local Machine zone, making this security zone a prime target for malicious users and code. Disabling or not configuring this setting could allow pages in the Internet zone to navigate to pages in the Local Machine zone to then run code to elevate privileges. This could allow malicious code or users to become active on user computers or the network.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Protection from Zone Elevation' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Protection from Zone Elevation' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Protection from Zone Elevation' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Protection from Zone Elevation' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Protection from Zone Elevation' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)

          V-223305 - ActiveX installation restriction must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223305r961779_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000023
          Identities:SV-108789
          V-99685
          CCI-002460 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: Microsoft ActiveX controls allow unmanaged, unprotected code to run on the user computers. ActiveX controls do not run within a protected container in the browser like the other types of HTML or Microsoft Silverlight-based controls. Disabling or not configuring this setting does not block prompts for ActiveX control installations, and these prompts display to users. This could allow malicious code to become active on user computers or the network.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Restrict ActiveX Install is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_restrict_activexinstall

          If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Restrict ActiveX Install to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223305
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000023 - ActiveX installation restriction must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:Microsoft ActiveX controls allow unmanaged, unprotected code to run on the user computers. ActiveX controls do not run within a protected container in the browser like the other types of HTML or Microsoft Silverlight-based controls. Disabling or not configuring this setting does not block prompts for ActiveX control installations, and these prompts display to users. This could allow malicious code to become active on user computers or the network.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for SharePoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict ActiveX Install' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Exchange)

          V-223306 - File Download Restriction must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223306r960921_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000024
          Identities:SV-108791
          V-99687
          CCI-001695 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (3); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (3).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (3); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (3))
          Description: Disabling this setting allows websites to present file download prompts via code without the user specifically initiating the download. User preferences may also allow the download to occur without prompting or interaction with the user. Even if Internet Explorer prompts the user to accept the download, some websites abuse this functionality. Malicious websites may continually prompt users to download a file or present confusing dialog boxes to trick users into downloading or running a file. If the download occurs and it contains malicious code, the code could become active on user computers or the network.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Restrict File Download is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_restrict_filedownload

          If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Restrict File Download to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223306
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000024 - File Download Restriction must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:Disabling this setting allows websites to present file download prompts via code without the user specifically initiating the download. User preferences may also allow the download to occur without prompting or interaction with the user. Even if Internet Explorer prompts the user to accept the download, some websites abuse this functionality. Malicious websites may continually prompt users to download a file or present confusing dialog boxes to trick users into downloading or running a file. If the download occurs and it contains malicious code, the code could become active on user computers or the network.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for SharePoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Restrict File Download' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Exchange)
