          V-223307 - The Save from URL feature must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223307r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000025
          Identities:SV-108793
          V-99689
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: Typically, when Internet Explorer loads a web page from a Universal Naming Convention (UNC) share that contains a Mark of the Web (MOTW) comment, indicating the page was saved from a site on the Internet, Internet Explorer runs the page in the Internet security zone instead of the less restrictive Local Intranet security zone. This functionality can be controlled separately for instances of Internet Explorer spawned by Office applications (for example, if a user clicks a link in an Office document or selects a menu option that loads a web page). If Internet Explorer does not evaluate the page for a MOTW, potentially dangerous code could be allowed to run.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Saved from URL is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_unc_savedfilecheck

          If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Saved from URL to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223307
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000025 - The Save from URL feature must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:Typically, when Internet Explorer loads a web page from a Universal Naming Convention (UNC) share that contains a Mark of the Web (MOTW) comment, indicating the page was saved from a site on the Internet, Internet Explorer runs the page in the Internet security zone instead of the less restrictive Local Intranet security zone. This functionality can be controlled separately for instances of Internet Explorer spawned by Office applications (for example, if a user clicks a link in an Office document or selects a menu option that loads a web page). If Internet Explorer does not evaluate the page for a MOTW, potentially dangerous code could be allowed to run.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Groove)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Powerpoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Sharepoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Powerpoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Exchange)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22330700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Saved from URL is set to 'Enabled' for Access
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22330700 (registry_object)

          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main\FeatureControl\Feature_unc_savedfilecheck'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'mse7.exe'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main\FeatureControl\Feature_unc_savedfilecheck\mse7.exe'. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223308 - Scripted Windows Security restrictions must be enabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223308r960921_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000026
          Identities:SV-108795
          V-99691
          CCI-001695 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (3); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (3).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (3); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (3))
          Description: Malicious websites often try to confuse or trick users into giving a site permission to perform an action allowing the site to take control of the users' computers in some manner. Disabling or not configuring this setting allows unknown websites to:
          - Create browser windows appearing to be from the local operating system.
          - Draw active windows displaying outside of the viewable areas of the screen capturing keyboard input.
          - Overlay parent windows with their own browser windows to hide important system information, choices, or prompts.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Scripted Window Security Restrictions is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main\FeatureControl\FEATURE_WINDOW_RESTRICTIONS

          If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >>Scripted Window Security Restrictions to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for all installed Office programs.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223308
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000026 - Scripted Windows Security restrictions must be enabled in all Office programs.
          Description:Malicious websites often try to confuse or trick users into giving a site permission to perform an action allowing the site to take control of the users' computers in some manner. Disabling or not configuring this setting allows unknown websites to:
          - Create browser windows appearing to be from the local operating system.
          - Draw active windows displaying outside of the viewable areas of the screen capturing keyboard input.
          - Overlay parent windows with their own browser windows to hide important system information, choices, or prompts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Access)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Access is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Excel)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Excel is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Groove and OneDrive)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (Microsoft OneDrive for Business 2016 is installed)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Outlook)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Outlook is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS PowerPoint is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Project)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Project is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Publisher)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Publisher is installed.)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Visio)
                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • false (MS Visio is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Word)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (MS Word is installed.)
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for OneNote)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND)) (negated)
                  • true (Microsoft OneNote 2016 is installed)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for SharePoint Designer)
                • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR)) (negated)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
                  • false (Check if the SharePoint Designer 2013 key is present)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (PowerPoint Viewer Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for PowerPoint Viewer)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Expressions Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Expressions)
              • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • true (Microsoft Exchange Install path) (negated)
                • false (IE Security 'Scripted Window Security Restrictions' is set to 'Enabled' and the check box is selected for Microsoft Exchange)

          V-223309 - Flash player activation must be disabled in all Office programs.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223309r961779_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-CO-000027
          Identities:SV-108797
          V-99693
          CCI-002460 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether the Adobe Flash control can be activated by Office documents. Note that activation blocking applies only within Office processes.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from three options to control whether and how Flash is blocked from activation:

          1. "Block all activation" prevents the Flash control from being loaded, whether directly referenced by the document or indirectly by another embedded object.
          2. "Block embedding/linking, allow other activation" prevents the Flash control from being loaded when directly referenced by the document, but does not prevent activation through another object.
          3. "Allow all activation" restores Office's default behavior, allowing the Flash control to be activated.

          Because this setting is not a true Group Policy setting and "tattoos" the registry, enabling the "Allow all activation" option is the only way to restore default behavior after either of the "Block" options has been applied. It is not recommended to configure this setting to "Disabled" or "Not Configured" after it has been enabled.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> Block Flash activation in Office documents >> Enabled >> Block all activation is set to "Enabled" Block all activation.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Office\Common\COM Compatibility

          If the value for COMMENT is REG_SZ = Block all Flash activation, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> Block Flash activation in Office documents >> Enabled >> Block all activation to "Enabled" (Block all activation).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223309
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-CO-000027 - Flash player activation must be disabled in all Office programs.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether the Adobe Flash control can be activated by Office documents. Note that activation blocking applies only within Office processes.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from three options to control whether and how Flash is blocked from activation:

          1. "Block all activation" prevents the Flash control from being loaded, whether directly referenced by the document or indirectly by another embedded object.
          2. "Block embedding/linking, allow other activation" prevents the Flash control from being loaded when directly referenced by the document, but does not prevent activation through another object.
          3. "Allow all activation" restores Office's default behavior, allowing the Flash control to be activated.

          Because this setting is not a true Group Policy setting and "tattoos" the registry, enabling the "Allow all activation" option is the only way to restore default behavior after either of the "Block" options has been applied. It is not recommended to configure this setting to "Disabled" or "Not Configured" after it has been enabled.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Flash player activation is disabled.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22330900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Flash player activation is disabled.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22330900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Office\Common\COM Compatibility'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'COMMENT'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:22330900 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to 'Block all Flash activation'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Office\Common\COM Compatibility\COMMENT'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223310 - Trusted Locations on the network must be disabled in Excel.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223310r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-EX-000001
          Identities:SV-108799
          V-99695
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether trusted locations on the network can be used.

          If you enable this policy setting, users can specify trusted locations on network shares or in other remote locations that are not under their direct control by selecting the "Allow Trusted Locations on my network (not recommended)" check box in the Trusted Locations section of the Trust Center. Content, code, and add-ins are allowed to load from trusted locations with minimal security and without prompting the user for permission.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the selected application ignores any network locations listed in the Trusted Locations section of the Trust Center. Disabling this policy setting does not delete any network locations from the Trusted Locations list. Instead, it forces the selected application to treat the locations as non-trusted and prevents users from adding new network locations to the list.

          If you also deploy Trusted Locations via Group Policy, you should verify whether any of them are remote locations. If any of them are remote locations and you do not allow remote locations via this policy setting, those policy keys that point to remote locations will be ignored on client computers.

          Disabling this policy setting will cause disruption for users who add network locations to the Trusted Locations list. However, it is not recommended to enable this policy setting (as the "Allow Trusted Locations on my network (not recommended)" check box itself states), so in practice it should be possible to disable this policy setting in most situations without causing significant usability issues for most users.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> Trusted Locations "Allow Trusted Locations on the network" is set to "Disabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\trusted locations

          If the value AllowNetworkLocations is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> Trusted Locations "Allow Trusted Locations on the network" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223310
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-EX-000001 - Trusted Locations on the network must be disabled in Excel.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether trusted locations on the network can be used.

          If you enable this policy setting, users can specify trusted locations on network shares or in other remote locations that are not under their direct control by selecting the "Allow Trusted Locations on my network (not recommended)" check box in the Trusted Locations section of the Trust Center. Content, code, and add-ins are allowed to load from trusted locations with minimal security and without prompting the user for permission.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the selected application ignores any network locations listed in the Trusted Locations section of the Trust Center. Disabling this policy setting does not delete any network locations from the Trusted Locations list. Instead, it forces the selected application to treat the locations as non-trusted and prevents users from adding new network locations to the list.

          If you also deploy Trusted Locations via Group Policy, you should verify whether any of them are remote locations. If any of them are remote locations and you do not allow remote locations via this policy setting, those policy keys that point to remote locations will be ignored on client computers.

          Disabling this policy setting will cause disruption for users who add network locations to the Trusted Locations list. However, it is not recommended to enable this policy setting (as the "Allow Trusted Locations on my network (not recommended)" check box itself states), so in practice it should be possible to disable this policy setting in most situations without causing significant usability issues for most users.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Trusted Locations on the network are disabled in Excel.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22331000 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Trusted Locations on the network are disabled in Excel.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22331000 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\trusted locations'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowNetworkLocations'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000021 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:

          V-223311 - VBA Macros not digitally signed must be blocked in Excel.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223311r960963_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-EX-000002
          Identities:SV-108801
          V-99697
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: This policy setting controls how the specified applications warn users when Visual Basic for Applications (VBA) macros are present.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from four options for determining how the specified applications will warn the user about macros:

          - Disable all with notification: The application displays the Trust Bar for all macros, whether signed or unsigned. This option enforces the default configuration in Office. This option also allows users to potentially enable unsigned/untrusted macros. If a site requires the use of macros, they must be signed /approved and added to appropriate locations listed in the Trust Center Settings.
          - Disable all except digitally signed macros: The application displays the Trust Bar for digitally signed macros, allowing users to enable them or leave them disabled. Any unsigned macros are disabled, and users are not notified.
          - Disable all without notification: The application disables all macros, whether signed or unsigned, and does not notify users.
          - Enable all macros (not recommended): All macros are enabled, whether signed or unsigned. This option can significantly reduce security by allowing dangerous code to run undetected.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> "Macro Notification Settings" is set to "Enabled" and "Disable VBA macros except digitally signed macros" from the Options is selected.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:
          HKCU\software\policies\Microsoft\office\16.0\excel\security

          If the value vbawarnings is REG_DWORD = 3, this is not a finding. A value of REG_DWORD = 4 is also acceptable. If the registry key does not exist or is not configured properly, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> "Macro Notification Settings" is set to "Enabled" and select "Disable VBA macros except digitally signed macros" from the Options.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223311
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-EX-000002 - VBA Macros not digitally signed must be blocked in Excel.
          Description:This policy setting controls how the specified applications warn users when Visual Basic for Applications (VBA) macros are present.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from four options for determining how the specified applications will warn the user about macros:

          - Disable all with notification: The application displays the Trust Bar for all macros, whether signed or unsigned. This option enforces the default configuration in Office.
          - Disable all except digitally signed macros: The application displays the Trust Bar for digitally signed macros, allowing users to enable them or leave them disabled. Any unsigned macros are disabled, and users are not notified.
          - Disable all without notification: The application disables all macros, whether signed or unsigned, and does not notify users.
          - Enable all macros (not recommended): All macros are enabled, whether signed or unsigned. This option can significantly reduce security by allowing dangerous code to run undetected.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Macro Notification Settings' is set to Enabled and 'Disable VBA macros except digitally signed macros' from the Options is selected.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22331100 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Macro Notification Settings' is set to Enabled and 'Disable VBA macros except digitally signed macros' from the Options is selected.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22331100 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'vbawarnings'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000024 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '3'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000025 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '4'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-1565594376-788080289-153769433-156119'

          V-223312 - Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch in Excel must be blocked.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223312r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-EX-000003
          Identities:SV-108803
          V-99699
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting allows you to control whether Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch is allowed.

          By default, DDE server launch is turned off, but users can turn on DDE server launch by going to File >> Options >> Trust Center >> Trust Center Settings >> External Content.

          For security reasons, turning on DDE server launch is not recommended.

          Note: For DDE server launch to work, Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup must be turned on. Be sure that the “Don't allow Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup” policy setting is not enabled, because enabling that policy setting turns off DDE server lookup.

          If you enable this policy setting, DDE server launch is not allowed, and users cannot turn on DDE server launch in the Trust Center.

          If you disable this policy setting, DDE server launch is allowed, and users cannot turn off DDE server launch in the Trust Center. For security reasons, this is not recommended.

          If you do not configure this policy setting, DDE server launch is turned off, but users can turn on DDE server launch in the Trust Center.

          Note: This policy setting only applies to subscription versions of Office, such as Office 365 ProPlus.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> External Content >> Don't allow Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch in Excel is set to "Enabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\excel\security\external content

          If the value for "disableddeserverlaunch" is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> External Content >> Don't allow Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch in Excel to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223312
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-EX-000003 - Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch in Excel must be blocked.
          Description:This policy setting allows you to control whether Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch is allowed.

          By default, DDE server launch is turned off, but users can turn on DDE server launch by going to File >> Options >> Trust Center >> Trust Center Settings >> External Content.

          For security reasons, turning on DDE server launch is not recommended.

          Note: For DDE server launch to work, Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup must be turned on. Be sure that the "Don't allow Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup" policy setting is not enabled, because enabling that policy setting turns off DDE server lookup.

          If you enable this policy setting, DDE server launch is not allowed, and users cannot turn on DDE server launch in the Trust Center.

          If you disable this policy setting, DDE server launch is allowed, and users cannot turn off DDE server launch in the Trust Center. For security reasons, this is not recommended.

          If you do not configure this policy setting, DDE server launch is turned off, but users can turn on DDE server launch in the Trust Center.

          Note: This policy setting only applies to subscription versions of Office, such as Office 365 ProPlus.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Don't allow Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch in Excel is Enabled.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22331200 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Don't allow Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch in Excel is Enabled.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22331200 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\external content'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'disableddeserverlaunch'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000022 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'

          V-223313 - Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup in Excel must be blocked.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223313r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-EX-000004
          Identities:SV-108805
          V-99701
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting allows you to control whether Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup is allowed.

          By default, DDE server lookup is turned on, but users can turn off DDE server lookup by going to File >> Options >> Trust Center >> Trust Center Settings >> External Content.

          If you enable this policy setting, DDE server lookup is not allowed, and users cannot turn on DDE server lookup in the Trust Center.

          Note: If you are using Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch, which is not recommended, do not enable this policy setting, because DDE server launch requires DDE server lookup to be on.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, DDE server lookup is turned on, but users can turn off DDE server lookup in the Trust Center.

          Note: This policy setting only applies to subscription versions of Office, such as Office 365 ProPlus.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> External Content >> Don't allow Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup in Excel is set to "Enabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\excel\security\external content

          If the value for "disableddeserverlookup" is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> External Content >> Don't allow Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup in Excel to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223313
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-EX-000004 - Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup in Excel must be blocked.
          Description:This policy setting allows you to control whether Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup is allowed.

          By default, DDE server lookup is turned on, but users can turn off DDE server lookup by going to File >> Options >> Trust Center >> Trust Center Settings >> External Content.

          If you enable this policy setting, DDE server lookup is not allowed, and users cannot turn on DDE server lookup in the Trust Center.

          Note: If you are using Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server launch, which is not recommended, do not enable this policy setting, because DDE server launch requires DDE server lookup to be on.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, DDE server lookup is turned on, but users can turn off DDE server lookup in the Trust Center.

          Note: This policy setting only applies to subscription versions of Office, such as Office 365 ProPlus.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup in Excel is blocked)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22331300 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) server lookup in Excel is blocked
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22331300 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\external content'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'disableddeserverlookup'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000022 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:

          V-223314 - Open/save of dBase III / IV format files must be blocked.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223314r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-EX-000005
          Identities:SV-108807
          V-99703
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files.

          The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "dBase III / IV files" is set to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock

          If the value DBaseFiles is REG_DWORD = 2, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "dBase III / IV files" to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223314
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-EX-000005 - Open/save of dBase III / IV format files must be blocked.
          Description:This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files.

          The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Open/save of dBase III / IV format files are blocked.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22331400 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Open/save of dBase III / IV format files are blocked.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22331400 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DBaseFiles'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000023 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'

          Description: This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Dif and Sylk files" is set to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock

          If the value DifandSylkFiles is REG_DWORD = 2, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Dif and Sylk files" to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.

          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          V-223316 - Open/save of Excel 2 macrosheets and add-in files must be blocked.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223316r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-EX-000007
          Identities:SV-108811
          V-99707
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 2 macrosheets and add-in files" is set to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock

          If the value XL2Macros is REG_DWORD = 2, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 2 macrosheets and add-in files" to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223316
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-EX-000007 - Open/save of Excel 2 macrosheets and add-in files must be blocked.
          Description:This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Excel 2 macrosheets and add-in files is set to Enabled:Open/Save blocked, use open policy.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22331600 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Excel 2 macrosheets and add-in files is set to Enabled:Open/Save blocked, use open policy.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22331600 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'xl2macros'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000023 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment:

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'

          Description: This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 2 worksheets" is set to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock

          If the value XL2Worksheets is REG_DWORD = 2, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 2 worksheets" to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.

          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 3 worksheets" is set to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock

          If the value XL3Worksheets is REG_DWORD = 2, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 3 worksheets" to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223319
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-EX-000010 - Open/save of Excel 3 worksheets must be blocked.
          Description:This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Excel 3 worksheets' is set to 'Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy')

          V-223320 - Open/save of Excel 4 macrosheets and add-in files must be blocked.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223320r961086_rule

          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel

          V-99715
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 4 macrosheets and add-in files" is set to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock

          If the value XL4Macros is REG_DWORD = 2, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 4 macrosheets and add-in files" to "Enabled: Open/Save blocked, use open policy".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0

          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          • days_since_modified equals '6'
          • filepath equals 'C:\Users\Administrator\ntuser.dat'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found in user profile ntuser.dat file(s): 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock\XL4Worksheets'. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          For 'MAXHEALTH\katessem.admin': Registry Name Not found in user profile ntuser.dat file(s): 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock\XL4Worksheets'.

          V-223323 - Open/save of Excel 95 workbooks must be blocked.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223323r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-EX-000014
          Identities:SV-108825
          V-99721
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings >> Excel 95 workbooks is set to "Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock

          If the value for xl95workbooks is REG_DWORD = 2, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings >> Excel 95 workbooks to "Open/Save blocked, use open policy".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223323
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-EX-000014 - Open/save of Excel 95 workbooks must be blocked.
          Description:This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Excel 95 workbooks is set to 'Open/Save blocked, use open policy')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22332300 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Excel 95 workbooks is set to 'Open/Save blocked, use open policy'
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22332300 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'xl95workbooks'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000023 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'

          V-223324 - Open/save of Excel 95-97 workbooks and templates must be blocked.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223324r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-EX-000015
          Identities:SV-108827
          V-99723
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting allows you to determine whether users can open, view, edit, or save Excel files with the format specified by the title of this policy setting. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 95-97 workbooks and templates" is set to "Open/Save blocked, use open policy".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\office\16.0\excel\security\fileblock

          If the value XL9597WorkbooksandTemplates is REG_DWORD = 2, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> File Block Settings "Excel 95-97 workbooks and templates" to "Open/Save blocked, use open policy".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0

If you enable this policy setting, you can specify whether users can open, view, edit, or save files. The options that can be selected are below. Note: Not all options may be available for this policy setting.

          - Do not block: The file type will not be blocked.
          - Save blocked: Saving of the file type will be blocked.
          - Open/Save blocked, use open policy: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked. The file will open based on the policy setting configured in the "default file block behavior" key.
          - Block: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the file will not open.
          - Open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit the file type will not be enabled.
          - Allow editing and open in Protected View: Both opening and saving of the file type will be blocked, and the option to edit will be enabled.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the file type will not be blocked.
