          V-223343 - File attachments from Outlook must be opened in Excel in Protected mode.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223343r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Excel
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-EX-000034
          Identities:SV-108865
          V-99761
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: This policy setting allows you to determine if Excel files in Outlook attachments open in Protected View.

          If you enable this policy setting, Outlook attachments do not open in Protected View.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook attachments open in Protected View.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> Protected View >> Turn off Protected View for attachments opened from Outlook is set to "Disabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\excel\security\protectedview

          If the value DisableAttachmentsInPV is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Excel 2016 >> Excel Options >> Security >> Trust Center >> Protected View >> Turn off Protected View for attachments opened from Outlook to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223343
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-EX-000034 - File attachments from Outlook must be opened in Excel in Protected mode.
          Description:This policy setting allows you to determine if Excel files in Outlook attachments open in Protected View.

          If you enable this policy setting, Outlook attachments do not open in Protected View.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook attachments open in Protected View.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Turn off Protected View for attachments opened from Outlook is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22334300 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Turn off Protected View for attachments opened from Outlook is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22334300 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\excel\security\protectedview'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableAttachmentsInPV'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'

          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'

          V-223344 - The SIP security mode in Lync must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223344r1043178_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Lync
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:O365-LY-000001
          Identities:SV-108867
          V-99763
          CCI-001184 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-23; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-23.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-23; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-23)
          Description: When Lync connects to the server, it supports various authentication mechanisms. This policy allows the user to specify whether Digest and Basic authentication are supported. Disabled (default): NTLM/Kerberos/TLS-DSK/Digest/Basic Enabled: Authentication mechanisms: NTLM/Kerberos/TLS-DSK Gal Download: Requires HTTPS if user is not logged in as an internal user.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Skype for Business 2016 >> Microsoft Lync Feature Policies "Configure SIP security mode" is set to "Enabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\office\16.0\lync

          If the value enablesiphighsecuritymode is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Skype for Business 2016 >> Microsoft Lync Feature Policies "Configure SIP security mode" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099

          V-223345 - The HTTP fallback for SIP connection in Lync must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223345r1043178_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Lync
          Rule Result:Not Applicable
          Version:O365-LY-000002
          Identities:SV-108869
          V-99765
          CCI-001184 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-23; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-23.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-23; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-23)
          Description: Prevents from HTTP being used for SIP connection in case TLS or TCP fail.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Skype for Business 2016 >> Microsoft Lync Feature Policies "Disable HTTP fallback for SIP connection" is set to "Enabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\office\16.0\lync

          If the value disablehttpconnect is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Skype for Business 2016 >> Microsoft Lync Feature Policies "Disable HTTP fallback for SIP connection" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099

          V-223346 - The Exchange client authentication with Exchange servers must be enabled to use Kerberos Password Authentication.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223346r961878_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000001
          Identities:SV-108871
          V-99767
          CCI-001967 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-3 (1))
          Description: This policy setting controls which authentication method Outlook uses to authenticate with Microsoft Exchange Server. Note: Exchange Server supports the Kerberos authentication protocol and NTLM for authentication. The Kerberos protocol is the more secure authentication method and is supported on Windows 2000 Server and later versions. NTLM authentication is supported in pre-Windows 2000 environments.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from three different options for controlling how Outlook authenticates with Microsoft Exchange Server:

          - Kerberos/NTLM password authentication. Outlook attempts to authenticate using the Kerberos authentication protocol. If this attempt fails, Outlook attempts to authenticate using NTLM. This option is the default configuration.
          - Kerberos password authentication. Outlook attempts to authenticate using the Kerberos protocol only.
          - NTLM password authentication. Outlook attempts to authenticate using NTLM only.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook will attempt to authenticate using the Kerberos authentication protocol. If it cannot (because no Windows 2000 or later domain controllers are available), it will authenticate using NTLM.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Account Settings >> Exchange >> Authentication with Exchange Server is set to Kerberos Password Authentication.

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value authenticationservice is set to REG_DWORD = 16 (decimal) or 10 (hex), this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Account Settings >> Exchange >> Authentication with Exchange Server to Kerberos Password Authentication.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223346
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000001 - The Exchange client authentication with Exchange servers must be enabled to use Kerberos Password Authentication.
          Description:This policy setting controls which authentication method Outlook uses to authenticate with Microsoft Exchange Server. Note: Exchange Server supports the Kerberos authentication protocol and NTLM for authentication. The Kerberos protocol is the more secure authentication method and is supported on Windows 2000 Server and later versions. NTLM authentication is supported in pre-Windows 2000 environments.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from three different options for controlling how Outlook authenticates with Microsoft Exchange Server:

          - Kerberos/NTLM password authentication. Outlook attempts to authenticate using the Kerberos authentication protocol. If this attempt fails, Outlook attempts to authenticate using NTLM. This option is the default configuration.
          - Kerberos password authentication. Outlook attempts to authenticate using the Kerberos protocol only.
          - NTLM password authentication. Outlook attempts to authenticate using NTLM only.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook will attempt to authenticate using the Kerberos authentication protocol. If it cannot (because no Windows 2000 or later domain controllers are available), it will authenticate using NTLM.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Outlook is configured to have Authentication with Exchange Server set to Kerberos Password Authentication.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22334600 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Outlook is configured to have Authentication with Exchange Server set to Kerberos Password Authentication.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22334600 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook\security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'authenticationservice'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:22334600 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '16'

          V-223347 - Outlook must use remote procedure call (RPC) encryption to communicate with Microsoft Exchange servers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223347r961878_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000002
          Identities:SV-108873
          V-99769
          CCI-001967 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-3 (1))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether Outlook uses remote procedure call (RPC) encryption to communicate with Microsoft Exchange servers.

          If you enable this policy setting, Outlook uses RPC encryption when communicating with an Exchange server. Note: RPC encryption only encrypts the data from the Outlook client computer to the Exchange server. It does not encrypt the messages themselves as they traverse the Internet.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, RPC encryption is still used by default. This setting allows you to override the corresponding per-profile setting.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Account Settings >> Exchange >> Enable RPC encryption is set to "Enabled".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\rpc

          If the value for enablerpcencryption is set to REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Account Settings >> Exchange >> Enable RPC encryption to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223347
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000002 - Outlook must use remote procedure call (RPC) encryption to communicate with Microsoft Exchange servers.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether Outlook uses remote procedure call (RPC) encryption to communicate with Microsoft Exchange servers.

          If you enable this policy setting, Outlook uses RPC encryption when communicating with an Exchange server. Note: RPC encryption only encrypts the data from the Outlook client computer to the Exchange server. It does not encrypt the messages themselves as they traverse the Internet.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, RPC encryption is still used by default. This setting allows you to override the corresponding per-profile setting.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Enable RPC encryption is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22334700 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Enable RPC encryption is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22334700 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook\rpc'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'enablerpcencryption'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000022 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment:

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'

          V-223348 - Scripts associated with public folders must be prevented from execution in Outlook.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223348r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000003

          V-99771
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether Outlook executes scripts that are associated with custom forms or folder home pages for public folders.
          Check Text:Verify the policy for Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Outlook Options >> Other >> Advanced >> Do not allow Outlook object model scripts to run for public folders is set to "Enabled".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value for publicfolderscript is set to REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Outlook Options >> Other >> Advanced >> Do not allow Outlook object model scripts to run for public folders to "Enabled".

          V-223349 - Scripts associated with shared folders must be prevented from execution in Outlook.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223349r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000004
          Identities:SV-108877
          V-99773
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether Outlook executes scripts associated with custom forms or folder home pages for shared folders.
          Check Text:Verify the policy for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Outlook Options >> Other >> Advanced >> Do not allow Outlook object model scripts to run for shared folders is set to "Enabled".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value for sharedfolderscript is set to REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Outlook Options >> Other >> Advanced >> Do not allow Outlook object model scripts to run for shared folders to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA

          V-223350 - Files dragged from an Outlook e-mail to the file system must be created in ANSI format.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223350r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000005
          Identities:SV-108879
          V-99775
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: This policy setting controls whether e-mail messages dragged from Outlook to the file system are saved in Unicode or ANSI format.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Outlook Options >> Other >> Advanced >> Use Unicode format when dragging e-mail message to file system is set to "Disabled".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\options\general

          If the value for msgformat is set to REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Outlook Options >> Other >> Advanced >> Use Unicode format when dragging e-mail message to file system to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA

          V-223352 - Active X One-Off forms must only be enabled to load with Outlook Controls.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223352r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000007
          Identities:SV-108883
          V-99779
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: By default, third-party ActiveX controls are not allowed to run in one-off forms in Outlook. You can change this behavior so that Safe Controls (Microsoft Forms 2.0 controls and the Outlook Recipient and Body controls) are allowed in one-off forms, or so that all ActiveX controls are allowed to run.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Allow Active X One Off Forms is set to "Enabled" "Load only Outlook Controls".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value for allowactivexoneoffforms is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Allow Active X One Off Forms to "Enabled" "Load only Outlook Controls".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223352
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000007 - Active X One-Off forms must only be enabled to load with Outlook Controls.
          Description:By default, third-party ActiveX controls are not allowed to run in one-off forms in Outlook. You can change this behavior so that Safe Controls (Microsoft Forms 2.0 controls and the Outlook Recipient and Body controls) are allowed in one-off forms, or so that all ActiveX controls are allowed to run.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Allow Active X One Off Forms is set to 'Enabled' and 'Load only Outlook Controls'.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22335200 (ntuser_test)

          Title:Allow Active X One Off Forms is set to 'Enabled' and 'Load only Outlook Controls'.

          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'

          V-223353 - Outlook must be configured to prevent users overriding attachment security settings.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223353r961353_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000008
          Identities:SV-108885
          V-99781
          CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: This policy setting prevents users from overriding the set of attachments blocked by Outlook.

          If you enable this policy setting users will be prevented from overriding the set of attachments blocked by Outlook. Outlook also checks the "Level1Remove" registry key when this setting is specified.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, users will be allowed to override the set of attachments blocked by Outlook.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Prevent users from customizing attachment security settings is set to "Enabled".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook

          If the value for disallowattachmentcustomization is set to REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Prevent users from customizing attachment security settings to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223353
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000008 - Outlook must be configured to prevent users overriding attachment security settings.
          Description:This policy setting prevents users from overriding the set of attachments blocked by Outlook.

          If you enable this policy setting users will be prevented from overriding the set of attachments blocked by Outlook. Outlook also checks the "Level1Remove" registry key when this setting is specified.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, users will be allowed to override the set of attachments blocked by Outlook.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Outlook is configured to prevent users overriding attachment security settings.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22335300 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Outlook is configured to prevent users overriding attachment security settings.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22335300 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'disallowattachmentcustomization'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000022 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'

          V-223354 - Internet must not be included in Safe Zone for picture download in Outlook.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223354r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000009
          Identities:SV-108887
          V-99783
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: This policy setting controls whether pictures and external content in HTML e-mail messages from untrusted senders on the Internet are downloaded without Outlook users explicitly choosing to do so.

          If you enable this policy setting, Outlook will automatically download external content in all e-mail messages sent over the Internet and users will not be able to change the setting.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook does not consider the Internet a safe zone, which means that Outlook will not automatically download content from external servers unless the sender is included in the Safe Senders list. Recipients can choose to download external content from untrusted senders on a message-by-message basis.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Automatic Picture Download Settings >> Include Internet in Safe Zones for Automatic Picture Download is set to "Disabled".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\options\mail

          If the value for Internet is set to REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Automatic Picture Download Settings >> Include Internet in Safe Zones for Automatic Picture Download to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223354
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000009 - Internet must not be included in Safe Zone for picture download in Outlook.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether pictures and external content in HTML e-mail messages from untrusted senders on the Internet are downloaded without Outlook users explicitly choosing to do so.

          If you enable this policy setting, Outlook will automatically download external content in all e-mail messages sent over the Internet and users will not be able to change the setting.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook does not consider the Internet a safe zone, which means that Outlook will not automatically download content from external servers unless the sender is included in the Safe Senders list. Recipients can choose to download external content from untrusted senders on a message-by-message basis.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))

          V-223355 - The Publish to Global Address List (GAL) button must be disabled in Outlook.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223355r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000010
          Identities:SV-108889
          V-99785
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: This policy setting controls whether Outlook users can publish e-mail certificates to the Global Address List (GAL).

          If you enable this policy setting, the "Publish to GAL" button does not display in the "E-mail Security" section of the Trust Center.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook users can publish their e-mail certificates to the GAL through the "E-mail Security" section of the Trust Center.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Cryptography >> Do not display 'Publish to GAL' button is set to "Enabled".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value for publishtogaldisabled is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Cryptography >> Do not display 'Publish to GAL' button to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223355
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000010 - The Publish to Global Address List (GAL) button must be disabled in Outlook.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether Outlook users can publish e-mail certificates to the Global Address List (GAL).

          If you enable this policy setting, the "Publish to GAL" button does not display in the "E-mail Security" section of the Trust Center.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook users can publish their e-mail certificates to the GAL through the "E-mail Security" section of the Trust Center.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Do not display 'Publish to GAL' button is set to 'Enabled'.)
          Tests:

          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'publishtogaldisabled'

          V-223356 - The minimum encryption key length in Outlook must be at least 168.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223356r1117184_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000011
          Identities:SV-108891
          V-99787
          CCI-002450 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-13; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-13 b)
          Description: This policy setting allows you to set the minimum key length for an encrypted e-mail message.

          If you enable this policy setting, you may set the minimum key length for an encrypted e-mail message. Outlook will display a warning dialog if the user tries to send a message using an encryption key that is below the minimum encryption key value set. The user can still choose to ignore the warning and send using the encryption key originally chosen.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, a dialog warning will be shown to the user if the user attempts to send a message using encryption. The user can still choose to ignore the warning and send using the encryption key originally chosen.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Cryptography >> Minimum encryption settings is set to "Enabled" and a Minimum key size (in bits) of "168" or above.

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value for minenckey is set to 168 or above, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Cryptography >> Minimum encryption settings to "Enabled"and a Minimum key size (in bits) of "168" or above.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223356
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000011 - The minimum encryption key length in Outlook must be at least 168.
          Description:This policy setting allows you to set the minimum key length for an encrypted e-mail message.

          If you enable this policy setting, you may set the minimum key length for an encrypted e-mail message. Outlook will display a warning dialog if the user tries to send a message using an encryption key that is below the minimum encryption key value set. The user can still choose to ignore the warning and send using the encryption key originally chosen.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, a dialog warning will be shown to the user if the user attempts to send a message using encryption. The user can still choose to ignore the warning and send using the encryption key originally chosen.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Outlook is configured to have the Minimum encryption settings set to 'Enabled' and a Minimum key size (in bits) of '168' or above.)

          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'minenckey'

          V-223357 - The warning about invalid digital signatures must be enabled to warn Outlook users.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223357r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000012
          Identities:SV-108893
          V-99789
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting controls how Outlook warns users about messages with invalid digital signatures.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from three options for controlling how Outlook users are warned about invalid signatures:
          - Let user decide if they want to be warned. This option enforces the default configuration.
          - Always warn about invalid signatures.
          - Never warn about invalid signatures.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, if users open e-mail messages that include invalid digital signatures, Outlook displays a warning dialog. Users can decide whether they want to be warned about invalid signatures in the future.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Cryptography >> Signature Warning is set to "Enabled" "Always warn about invalid signatures".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value for warnaboutinvalid is set to REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Cryptography >> Signature Warning to "Enabled" "Always warn about invalid signatures".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223357
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000012 - The warning about invalid digital signatures must be enabled to warn Outlook users.
          Description:This policy setting controls how Outlook warns users about messages with invalid digital signatures.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from three options for controlling how Outlook users are warned about invalid signatures:
          - Let user decide if they want to be warned. This option enforces the default configuration.
          - Always warn about invalid signatures.
          - Never warn about invalid signatures.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, if users open e-mail messages that include invalid digital signatures, Outlook displays a warning dialog. Users can decide whether they want to be warned about invalid signatures in the future.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Outlook users are always warned about invalid signatures.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22335700 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Outlook users are always warned about invalid signatures.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook\security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'warnaboutinvalid'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'

          V-223358 - Outlook must be configured to allow retrieving of Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs) always when online.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223358r961893_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000013
          Identities:SV-108895
          V-99791
          CCI-000185 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (2) (a); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (2) (b) (1))
          Description: This policy setting controls how Outlook retrieves Certificate Revocation Lists to verify the validity of certificates. Certificate revocation lists (CRLs) are lists of digital certificates that have been revoked by their controlling certificate authorities (CAs), typically because the certificates were issued improperly or their associated private keys were compromised.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from three options to govern how Outlook uses CRLs:
          - Use system Default. Outlook relies on the CRL download schedule that is configured for the operating system.
          - When online always retrieve the CRL. This option is the default configuration in Outlook.
          - Never retrieve the CRL. Outlook will not attempt to download the CRL for a certificate, even if it is online. This option can reduce security.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, when Outlook handles a certificate that includes a URL from which a CRL can be downloaded, Outlook will retrieve the CRL from the provided URL if Outlook is online.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Cryptography >> Signature Status dialog box >> Retrieving CRLs (Certificate Revocation Lists) is set to "Enabled" "When online always retrieve the CRL".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value for usecrlchasing is set to REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Cryptography >> Signature Status dialog box >> Retrieving CRLs (Certificate Revocation Lists) to "Enabled" "When online always retrieve the CRL".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223358
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000013 - Outlook must be configured to allow retrieving of Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs) always when online.
          Description:This policy setting controls how Outlook retrieves Certificate Revocation Lists to verify the validity of certificates. Certificate revocation lists (CRLs) are lists of digital certificates that have been revoked by their controlling certificate authorities (CAs), typically because the certificates were issued improperly or their associated private keys were compromised.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from three options to govern how Outlook uses CRLs:
          - Use system Default. Outlook relies on the CRL download schedule that is configured for the operating system.
          - When online always retrieve the CRL. This option is the default configuration in Outlook.
          - Never retrieve the CRL. Outlook will not attempt to download the CRL for a certificate, even if it is online. This option can reduce security.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, when Outlook handles a certificate that includes a URL from which a CRL can be downloaded, Outlook will retrieve the CRL from the provided URL if Outlook is online.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (In Outlook the Retrieving CRLs (Certificate Revocation Lists) is set to 'Enabled' 'When online always retrieve the CRL'.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22335800 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:In Outlook the Retrieving CRLs (Certificate Revocation Lists) is set to 'Enabled' 'When online always retrieve the CRL'.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22335800 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook\security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'usecrlchasing'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000022 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:

          V-223359 - The Outlook Security Mode must be enabled to always use the Outlook Security Group Policy.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223359r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000014
          Identities:SV-108897
          V-99793
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: This policy setting controls which set of security settings are enforced in Outlook. If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from four options for enforcing Outlook security settings:

          - Outlook Default Security - This option is the default configuration in Outlook. Users can configure security themselves, and Outlook ignores any security-related settings configured in Group Policy.
          - Use Security Form from "Outlook Security Settings" Public Folder - Outlook uses the settings from the security form published in the designated public folder.
          - Use Security Form from "Outlook 10 Security Settings" Public Folder - Outlook uses the settings from the security form published in the designated public folder.
          - Use Outlook Security Group Policy - Outlook uses security settings from Group Policy. Important: You must enable this policy setting if you want to apply the other Outlook security policy settings mentioned in this guide. If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook users can configure security for themselves, and Outlook ignores any security-related settings that are configured in Group Policy.

          Note: In previous versions of Outlook, when security settings were published in a form in Exchange Server public folders, users who needed these settings required the HKEY_CURRENT_USER >> Software >> Policies >> Microsoft >> Security >> CheckAdminSettings registry key to be set on their computers for the settings to apply. In Outlook, the CheckAdminSettings registry key is no longer used to determine users' security settings. Instead, the Outlook Security Mode setting can be used to determine whether Outlook security should be controlled directly by Group Policy, by the security form from the Outlook Security Settings Public Folder, or by the settings on users' own computers.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Outlook Security Mode is set to "Enabled (Use Outlook Security Group Policy)".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value for adminsecuritymode is set to REG_DWORD = 3, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Outlook Security Mode to "Enabled (Use Outlook Security Group Policy)".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223359
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000014 - The Outlook Security Mode must be enabled to always use the Outlook Security Group Policy.
          Description:This policy setting controls which set of security settings are enforced in Outlook. If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from four options for enforcing Outlook security settings:

          - Outlook Default Security - This option is the default configuration in Outlook. Users can configure security themselves, and Outlook ignores any security-related settings configured in Group Policy.
          - Use Security Form from "Outlook Security Settings" Public Folder - Outlook uses the settings from the security form published in the designated public folder.
          - Use Security Form from "Outlook 10 Security Settings" Public Folder - Outlook uses the settings from the security form published in the designated public folder.
          - Use Outlook Security Group Policy - Outlook uses security settings from Group Policy. Important: You must enable this policy setting if you want to apply the other Outlook security policy settings mentioned in this guide. If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook users can configure security for themselves, and Outlook ignores any security-related settings that are configured in Group Policy.

          Note: In previous versions of Outlook, when security settings were published in a form in Exchange Server public folders, users who needed these settings required the HKEY_CURRENT_USER >> Software >> Policies >> Microsoft >> Security >> CheckAdminSettings registry key to be set on their computers for the settings to apply. In Outlook, the CheckAdminSettings registry key is no longer used to determine users' security settings. Instead, the Outlook Security Mode setting can be used to determine whether Outlook security should be controlled directly by Group Policy, by the security form from the Outlook Security Settings Public Folder, or by the settings on users' own computers.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Outlook Security Mode is set to 'Enabled (Use Outlook Security Group Policy)')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22335900 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Outlook Security Mode is set to 'Enabled (Use Outlook Security Group Policy)'
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.

          V-223360 - The ability to demote attachments from Level 2 to Level 1 must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223360r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000015
          Identities:SV-108899
          V-99795
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether Outlook users can demote attachments to Level 2 by using a registry key, which will allow them to save files to disk and open them from that location. Outlook uses two levels of security to restrict access to files attached to e-mail messages or other items. Files with specific extensions can be categorized as Level 1 (users cannot view the file) or Level 2 (users can open the file after saving it to disk). Users can freely open files of types that are not categorized as Level 1 or Level 2.

          If you enable this policy setting, users can create a list of Level 1 file types to demote to Level 2 by adding the file types to the following registry key: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\14.0\Outlook\Security\Level1Remove.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, users cannot demote level 1 attachments to level 2, and the HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\14.0\Outlook\Security\Level1Remove registry key has no effect.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Attachment Security >> Allow users to demote attachments to Level 2 is set to "Disabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value allowuserstolowerattachments is set to REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Attachment Security >> Allow users to demote attachments to Level 2 to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223360
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000015 - The ability to demote attachments from Level 2 to Level 1 must be disabled.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether Outlook users can demote attachments to Level 2 by using a registry key, which will allow them to save files to disk and open them from that location. Outlook uses two levels of security to restrict access to files attached to e-mail messages or other items. Files with specific extensions can be categorized as Level 1 (users cannot view the file) or Level 2 (users can open the file after saving it to disk). Users can freely open files of types that are not categorized as Level 1 or Level 2.

          If you enable this policy setting, users can create a list of Level 1 file types to demote to Level 2 by adding the file types to the following registry key: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\14.0\Outlook\Security\Level1Remove.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, users cannot demote level 1 attachments to level 2, and the HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\14.0\Outlook\Security\Level1Remove registry key has no effect.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Allow users to demote attachments to Level 2 is set to 'Disabled'.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22336000 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Allow users to demote attachments to Level 2 is set to 'Disabled'.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22336000 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook\security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'allowuserstolowerattachments'
          Exclude Items If:

          V-223361 - The display of Level 1 attachments must be disabled in Outlook.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223361r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000016
          Identities:SV-108901
          V-99797
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether Outlook blocks potentially dangerous attachments designated Level 1. Outlook uses two levels of security to restrict users' access to files attached to e-mail messages or other items. Files with specific extensions can be categorized as Level 1 (users cannot view the file) or Level 2 (users can open the file after saving it to disk). Users can freely open files of types that are not categorized as Level 1 or Level 2.

          If you enable this policy setting, Outlook users can gain access to Level 1 file type attachments by first saving the attachments to disk and then opening them, as with Level 2 attachments. If you disable this policy setting, Level 1 attachments do not display under any circumstances. If you do not configure this policy setting, Outlook completely blocks access to Level 1 files, and requires users to save Level 2 files to disk before opening them.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Attachment Security >> Display Level 1 attachments is set to "Disabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value ShowLevel1Attach is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Attachment Security "Display Level 1 attachments" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223361
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000016 - The display of Level 1 attachments must be disabled in Outlook.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether Outlook blocks potentially dangerous attachments designated Level 1. Outlook uses two levels of security to restrict users' access to files attached to e-mail messages or other items. Files with specific extensions can be categorized as Level 1 (users cannot view the file) or Level 2 (users can open the file after saving it to disk). Users can freely open files of types that are not categorized as Level 1 or Level 2.

          If you enable this policy setting, Outlook users can gain access to Level 1 file type attachments by first saving the attachments to disk and then opening them, as with Level 2 attachments. If you disable this policy setting, Level 1 attachments do not display under any circumstances. If you do not configure this policy setting, Outlook completely blocks access to Level 1 files, and requires users to save Level 2 files to disk before opening them.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The display of Level 1 attachments is disabled in Outlook.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22336100 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The display of Level 1 attachments is disabled in Outlook.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment:

          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'showlevel1attach'

          V-223362 - Level 1 file attachments must be blocked from being delivered.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223362r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:O365-OU-000017
          Identities:SV-108903
          V-99799
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether Outlook users can demote attachments to Level 2 by using a registry key, which will allow them to save files to disk and open them from that location. Outlook uses two levels of security to restrict access to files attached to email messages or other items. Files with specific extensions can be categorized as Level 1 (users cannot view the file) or Level 2 (users can open the file after saving it to disk). Users can freely open files of types that are not categorized as Level 1 or Level 2.

          If you enable this policy setting, users can create a list of Level 1 file types to demote to Level 2 by adding the file types to the following registry key: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Outlook\Security\Level1Remove.

          If this policy setting is disabled or not configured, users cannot demote Level 1 attachments to Level 2, and the HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Outlook\Security\Level1Remove registry key has no effect.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Attachment Security >> Remove file extensions blocked as Level 1 is set to "Disabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security\FileExtensionsRemoveLevel1

          If the registry key exists, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Attachment Security >> Remove file extensions blocked as Level 1 to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223362
          Definition Result:true
          Title:O365-OU-000017 - Level 1 file attachments must be blocked from being delivered.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether Outlook users can demote attachments to Level 2 by using a registry key, which will allow them to save files to disk and open them from that location. Outlook uses two levels of security to restrict access to files attached to email messages or other items. Files with specific extensions can be categorized as Level 1 (users cannot view the file) or Level 2 (users can open the file after saving it to disk). Users can freely open files of types that are not categorized as Level 1 or Level 2.

          If you enable this policy setting, users can create a list of Level 1 file types to demote to Level 2 by adding the file types to the following registry key: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Outlook\Security\Level1Remove.

          If this policy setting is disabled or not configured, users cannot demote Level 1 attachments to Level 2, and the HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Outlook\Security\Level1Remove registry key has no effect.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (In Outlook Remove file extensions blocked as Level 1 is set to 'Disabled')

          V-223363 - Level 2 file attachments must be blocked from being delivered.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223363r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Pass
          Version:O365-OU-000018
          Identities:SV-108905
          V-99801
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting controls which types of attachments (determined by file extension) must be saved to disk before users can open them. Files with specific extensions can be categorized as Level 1 (users cannot view the file) or Level 2 (users can open the file after saving it to disk). Users can freely open files of types that are not categorized as Level 1 or Level 2.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can specify a list of attachment file types to classify as Level 2, which forces users to actively decide to download the attachment to view it.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook does not classify any file type extensions as Level 2.

          Important: This policy setting only applies if the "Outlook Security Mode" policy setting under "Microsoft Outlook 2016\Security\Security Form Settings" is configured to "Use Outlook Security Group Policy".
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Attachment Security >> Remove file extensions blocked as Level 2 is set to "Disabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security\FileExtensionsRemoveLevel2

          If the registry key exists, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Attachment Security >> Remove file extensions blocked as Level 2 to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223363
          Definition Result:true
          Title:O365-OU-000018 - Level 2 file attachments must be blocked from being delivered.
          Description:This policy setting controls which types of attachments (determined by file extension) must be saved to disk before users can open them. Files with specific extensions can be categorized as Level 1 (users cannot view the file) or Level 2 (users can open the file after saving it to disk). Users can freely open files of types that are not categorized as Level 1 or Level 2.

          If you enable this policy setting, you can specify a list of attachment file types to classify as Level 2, which forces users to actively decide to download the attachment to view it.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook does not classify any file type extensions as Level 2.

          Important: This policy setting only applies if the "Outlook Security Mode" policy setting under "Microsoft Outlook 2016\Security\Security Form Settings" is configured to "Use Outlook Security Group Policy".
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • true (In Outlook Remove file extensions blocked as Level 2 is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22336300 (ntuser_test)
          Result:true
          Title:In Outlook Remove file extensions blocked as Level 2 is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:No collected items may exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:22336300 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook\security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'FileExtensionsRemoveLevel2'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          Exclude Items If:
          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'

          V-223364 - Outlook must be configured to not run scripts in forms in which the script and the layout are contained within the message.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223364r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000019
          Identities:SV-108907
          V-99803
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether scripts can run in Outlook forms in which the script and layout are contained within the message. If you enable this policy setting, scripts can run in one-off Outlook forms. If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook does not run scripts in forms in which the script and the layout are contained within the message. Important: This policy setting only applies if the "Outlook Security Mode" policy setting under "Microsoft Outlook 2016\Security\Security Form Settings" is configured to "Use Outlook Security Group Policy".
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Custom Form Security "Allow scripts in one-off Outlook forms" is set to "Disabled".

          Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value EnableOneOffFormScripts is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Custom Form Security "Allow scripts in one-off Outlook forms" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223364
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000019 - Outlook must be configured to not run scripts in forms in which the script and the layout are contained within the message.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether scripts can run in Outlook forms in which the script and layout are contained within the message. If you enable this policy setting, scripts can run in one-off Outlook forms. If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, Outlook does not run scripts in forms in which the script and the layout are contained within the message. Important: This policy setting only applies if the "Outlook Security Mode" policy setting under "Microsoft Outlook 2016\Security\Security Form Settings" is configured to "Use Outlook Security Group Policy".
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Custom Form Security 'Allow scripts in one-off Outlook forms' is set to 'Disabled'.)
          Tests:

          V-223365 - When a custom action is executed that uses the Outlook object model, Outlook must automatically deny it.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-223365r961779_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:MS_Office_365_ProPlus_Outlook
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:O365-OU-000020
          Identities:SV-108909
          V-99805
          CCI-002460 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: This policy setting controls whether Outlook prompts users before executing a custom action. Custom actions add functionality to Outlook that can be triggered as part of a rule. Among other possible features, custom actions can be created that reply to messages in ways that circumvent the Outlook model's programmatic send protections. If this policy is enabled, four options are available to control how Outlook functions when a custom action is executed that uses the Outlook object model:
          - Prompt User
          - Automatically Approve
          - Automatically Deny
          - Prompt user based on computer security. This option enforces the default configuration in Outlook.

          If this policy is disabled or not configured, when Outlook or another program initiates a custom action using the Outlook object model, users are prompted to allow or reject the action. If this configuration is changed, malicious code can use the Outlook object model to compromise sensitive information or otherwise cause data and computing resources to be at risk. This is the equivalent of choosing Enabled -- Prompt user based on computer security.
          Check Text:Verify the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Custom Form Security >> Set Outlook object model custom actions execution prompt is set to "Enabled" and "Automatically Deny".

          Use the Windows Registry to navigate to the following key:

          HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\outlook\security

          If the value for "promptoomcustomaction" is set to "REG_DWORD = 0", this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Outlook 2016 >> Security >> Security Form Settings >> Custom Form Setting >> Set Outlook object model custom actions execution prompt to "Enabled" and select "Automatically Deny".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Office 365 ProPlus
          Identifier:4099
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.office365:def:223365
          Definition Result:false
          Title:O365-OU-000020 - When a custom action is executed that uses the Outlook object model, Outlook must automatically deny it.
          Description:This policy setting controls whether Outlook prompts users before executing a custom action. Custom actions add functionality to Outlook that can be triggered as part of a rule. Among other possible features, custom actions can be created that reply to messages in ways that circumvent the Outlook model's programmatic send protections. If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from four options to control how Outlook functions when a custom action is executed that uses the Outlook object model:
          - Prompt User
          - Automatically Approve
          - Automatically Deny
          - Prompt user based on computer security. This option enforces the default configuration in Outlook.

          If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, when Outlook or another program initiates a custom action using the Outlook object model, users are prompted to allow or reject the action. If this configuration is changed, malicious code can use the Outlook object model to compromise sensitive information or otherwise cause data and computing resources to be at risk. This is the equivalent of choosing Enabled -- Prompt user based on computer security.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (When a custom action is executed that uses the Outlook object model, Outlook automatically denies it.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:22336500 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:When a custom action is executed that uses the Outlook object model, Outlook automatically denies it.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment:

          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\outlook\security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'promptoomcustomaction'

          • days_since_modified is greater than '35'
